title: Stop redundant derived-state recomputation across dashboard renders
prompt: Profile `RevenueDashboard` through its React-style `getRenderProfile()` counters and fix the focused render regression in `src/revenueDashboard.ts`. A fresh parent render must reuse the last table view when the table dependencies are unchanged: `snapshot.rows` by identity, `minimumRevenue` by value, and `currency` by value. Parent-only changes such as `snapshot.receivedAt` or `selectedAccountId` must still appear in the returned dashboard view without rendering `RevenueTable` or recomputing its expensive derived state. Every fresh dependency change must render and recompute the table exactly once. Preserve the existing stale-data contract: after a fresh result exists, stale snapshots keep showing that last fresh table, do not update the memoized table dependencies, and still expose current outer stale metadata; the next fresh snapshot must apply all current dependencies. Do not modify the protected tests.
